$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

value.description: The value at the given location

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

group_id.description: Set when this grant is inherited from a group the principal belongs to (the group's id); absent for a direct grant, which is the only kind revocable from the principal.

group_name.description: The source group's label, present when the grant is inherited.

scope_op.description: How the scope root matches the tree: subtree (root + descendants), subtree_excl_root (descendants only for update/delete, root kept for read/create), or self (the root row only). Empty means subtree. Moot for the all scope.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

grant_count.description: How many grants the group confers on its members.

member_count.description: How many principals belong to the group (populated on list and get; 0 from create/update).

db.description: Database leg via the Storage Gateway: ok or down

status.description: Overall health: ok when all legs pass, degraded otherwise

has_avatar.description: True when the principal has a profile picture; fetch it from the avatar endpoint.

must_change_password.description: True when an admin reset the password and the user must change it before doing anything else; the console gates every route to the change-password form until it clears.

mode.description: view_as is read-only; act_as is full, with mutations attributed to both the real actor and the impersonated principal

secret.description: Whether the field is encrypted at rest and masked here

origin.description: operator (set at creation) or lifecycle (filled by the secret's own machinery)

current.description: True for the credential that authenticated this request; revoking it signs out the current session

expires_at.description: When the credential expires (RFC 3339); every credential is now time-bounded

kind.description: session for a web login, token for a CLI/API credential (omniglass token)

prefix.description: The non-secret ogp locator, so a credential is recognizable without exposing the token

get.description: Recent audit-trail events, newest first, each with the actor and, for an impersonated action, the real actor behind it. Read-only; gated by audit:read:admin (admin/owner only, since the audit trail is admin-sensitive).

parameters.description: Max rows to return, newest first (default 100, capped at 500)
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max rows to return, newest first (default 100, capped at 500)

parameters.description: Filter to one resource kind (e.g. auth, principal_grant)
parameters.name: resource

schema.description: Filter to one resource kind (e.g. auth, principal_grant)

parameters.description: Filter to one verb (e.g. login, create)
parameters.name: verb

schema.description: Filter to one verb (e.g. login, create)

parameters.description: Only events strictly older than this RFC3339 timestamp (paging backward)
parameters.name: before

schema.description: Only events strictly older than this RFC3339 timestamp (paging backward)

post.description: Verifies a human's password and sets an httpOnly session cookie. Public; a bad credential is a flat 401, and a correct password against a disabled account is a distinct 403 so the screen can explain it.
post.summary: Log in with a username and password

post.description: Revokes the session token and clears the cookie. Public.

get.description: Returns the caller's principal, flattened permissions (a UI hint and the fast-reject set), and grants. Requires authentication.
get.summary: The authenticated principal, its permissions, and grants

patch.description: Updates the caller's own display name (email is administrator-set). Requires authentication; self-scoped (edits only your own principal).

get.description: Returns the caller's profile picture as a base64-encoded JPEG. Requires authentication; self-scoped. No picture is a 404.

Lists the caller's own active bearer credentials (time-bounded web-login sessions and CLI/API tokens) with their non-secret metadata; the current one is flagged. Requires authentication; self-scoped (never another principal's). The token secret is never returned.
get.summary: List your own sessions and tokens

post.description: Revokes one of the caller's own sessions or tokens by id (from the session list); revoking the current one signs it out. Requires authentication; self-scoped, so a credential id that is not yours is a 404.

parameters.description: The credential id to revoke (from the session list)
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The credential id to revoke (from the session list)

post.summary: Revoke one of your own sessions

post.description: Revokes every one of the caller's own web-login sessions, or every one of its CLI/API tokens (chosen by purpose), returning how many were ended. Requires authentication; self-scoped. Always keeps the credential that made this request, so you are never signed out of the one you are on; sessions and tokens never cross.
post.summary: Revoke all of your own sessions or tokens

post.description: Verifies the current password and sets a new one. Requires authentication; self-scoped.
Clears the caller's profile picture. Requires authentication; self-scoped.
Sets the caller's profile picture (JPEG, PNG, or WebP, base64-encoded), normalized server-side to a 256x256 JPEG. Requires authentication; self-scoped. A bad or oversize image is a 422.
Revokes the impersonation session presented by the request token, ending the view-as / act-as. Requires an impersonation token.

get.description: Public: reports whether any owner has been bootstrapped, so the login screen can hide the bootstrap hint.
get.summary: Whether the system has an owner yet

get.description: Lists the components the caller may read, each filtered to its scope subtree. Gated by component:read.

post.description: Creates a component, optionally under a parent (a root needs an all-scoped grant), bound to a system and a location. Gated by component:create.

delete.description: Deletes a component, refused while it still has child components. Gated by component:delete; read and delete scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches a component by name within the caller's read scope. Out of scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by component:read.

patch.description: Patches a component's display_name or component_type. Gated by component:update; read and update scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Resolves the secrets that cascade onto a component (global -> location -> system -> component, most-specific winning), each masked, winner and shadowed candidates. Gated by secret:read; the component must be in the caller's component read scope.

Resolves the tags that cascade onto a component (global -> location -> system -> component): keys union, values override most-specific-wins, with the winner and shadowed candidates. A non-propagating key resolves only from a binding on the component itself. Gated by component:read; the component must be in the caller's component read scope.

Resolves the variables that cascade onto a component (global -> location -> system -> component, most-specific winning), winner and shadowed candidates. Gated by variable:read; the component must be in the caller's component read scope.
Lists the tags bound directly on a component (not the resolved cascade). Gated by component:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a component. Gated by component:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a component

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a component. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by component:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a component

get.description: Reports process health and the database leg, pinged through the Storage Gateway.
Lists the location_type registry (the shape-definers a location is classified by), ordered by rank. Populates the type picker on the location form. Gated by location:read.
Lists the locations the caller may read, each filtered to its scope subtree. Gated by location:read.

post.description: Creates a location, optionally under a parent (a root needs an all-scoped grant). Gated by location:create.

delete.description: Deletes a location, refused while it still has child locations. Gated by location:delete; read and delete scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches a location by name within the caller's read scope. Out of scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by location:read.

patch.description: Patches a location's display_name or location_type. Gated by location:update; the read and update scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Lists the tags bound directly on a location (not the resolved cascade). Gated by location:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a location. Gated by location:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a location

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a location. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by location:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a location

get.description: Every principal group. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

post.description: Creates a principal group. Gated by principal_group:create (all-scope). A duplicate name is 409.

delete.description: Removes a group and, by cascade, its memberships and grants. Gated by principal_group:delete (all-scope).

get.description: One principal group by id. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

patch.description: Updates a group's name and presentational fields. Gated by principal_group:update (all-scope). A duplicate name is 409.

get.description: The role x scope grants a group confers on its members. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

post.description: Assigns a role at a scope to a group; its members inherit it. Gated by principal_grant:create (all-scope). Refused (403) when the granted role's capabilities exceed the granter's own, exactly as for a direct grant. A duplicate is 409.
post.summary: Grant a role to a group

delete.description: Removes one grant from a group. Gated by principal_grant:delete (all-scope).

get.description: The principals in a group. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

post.description: Adds a principal to a group; its members inherit the group's grants. Gated by principal_group:update (all-scope). Idempotent.
post.summary: Add a member to a group

delete.description: Removes a principal from a group; it stops inheriting the group's grants. Gated by principal_group:update (all-scope).
delete.summary: Remove a member from a group

get.description: Lists all principals (humans and service accounts) with their grants. Gated by principal:read:admin.

parameters.description: Include archived (soft-deleted) principals, hidden by default
parameters.name: include_archived

schema.description: Include archived (soft-deleted) principals, hidden by default

post.description: Creates a human principal with an optional initial password. Gated by principal:create (all-scope). The new principal holds no grants; assign roles separately.

get.description: Fetches one principal by id with its profile and grants. Gated by principal:read:admin.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

patch.description: Updates a human principal's display name, email, and username. Gated by principal:update (all-scope). Renaming is safe: nothing keys on the username.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

get.description: Returns the principal's profile picture as a base64-encoded JPEG. Gated by principal:read:admin. A principal without a picture is a 404.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Assigns a role at a scope to a principal. Gated by principal_grant:create (all-scope). Refused (403) when the granted role's capabilities exceed the granter's own (no promoting anyone, including yourself, to a higher tier such as owner). A duplicate is 409, an unknown role or bad scope 422.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.summary: Grant a role to a principal

delete.description: Removes one grant from a principal. Gated by principal_grant:delete (all-scope). The last owner grant cannot be revoked.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

get.description: Lists another principal's active bearer credentials (login sessions and API tokens) with their non-secret metadata, newest first, so an administrator can see where an account is signed in and revoke a session that should not be. Gated by principal:revoke-session (all-scope). The token secret is never returned, and current is always false (there is no "this request's own session" when viewing another principal).

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Revokes one of another principal's sessions or tokens by id (an administrator action; the target is immediately signed out of that credential). Gated by principal:revoke-session (all-scope). Bounded to the target, so a credential id that is not theirs is a non-disclosing 404, never a cross-principal revoke. Refused (403) on an owner (an owner's sessions cannot be revoked by anyone, the takeover guard shared with impersonation and password reset) or when it would exceed the caller's own capabilities. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

parameters.description: The credential id to revoke (from the principal's session list)
parameters.name: sid

schema.description: The credential id to revoke (from the principal's session list)

post.description: Revokes every one of another principal's web-login sessions, or every one of its CLI/API tokens (chosen by purpose), in a single administrator action, returning how many were ended. Gated by principal:revoke-session (all-scope). Bounded to the target and never crosses purpose (revoking sessions leaves tokens, and vice versa). Refused (403) on an owner (the takeover guard shared with impersonation and the password reset) or when it would exceed the caller's own capabilities. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.summary: Revoke all of a principal's sessions or tokens

post.description: Soft-deletes a principal: it is hidden from the directory, can no longer authenticate, and its rows stay intact, reversibly (restore) until purged. Gated by principal:archive (all-scope). The last active owner cannot be archived.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Soft-disables a principal so it can no longer authenticate; its audit trail is kept. Gated by principal:update (all-scope). The last active owner cannot be disabled.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Re-enables a disabled principal, restoring its ability to authenticate. Gated by principal:update (all-scope).

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Mints a bounded, revocable token to view as (read-only) or act as (full) the target. Gated by principal:impersonate (all-scope). Refused on self, on an owner target (owners are un-impersonatable by anyone), when it would grant a capability the caller lacks (the escalation guard), or from within an existing impersonation.

parameters.description: The principal to impersonate, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal to impersonate, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Hard-deletes an archived principal and its owned rows (profile, credentials, grants, memberships); the audit trail is preserved. Irreversible. Gated by principal:purge (admin-sensitive, all-scope), and the principal must be archived first.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Clears another human principal's profile picture. Gated by principal:set-avatar (all-scope). Removing an absent picture is a no-op. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Sets a new password for another human principal (an administrator action; the target's current password is not required). Gated by principal:reset-password (all-scope). The new password must meet the password policy; a violation is a 422. Refused on yourself (change your own password from your profile, which verifies your current one), on an owner (owners cannot be reset by anyone), or when it would exceed the caller's own capabilities (the takeover guard, shared with impersonation). The action is audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Reverses an archive: the account is restored to active and can authenticate again. Gated by principal:archive (all-scope).

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Sets another human principal's profile picture (an administrator action). Gated by principal:set-avatar (all-scope). The image (JPEG, PNG, or WebP, base64-encoded) is normalized server-side to a 256x256 JPEG; a bad or oversize image is a 422. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

get.description: Lists the roles with their metadata and effective (flattened) permissions. Gated by the role:read:admin capability.
Lists the secret_type shapes a secret can take, for the create form. Gated by secret:read.
Lists every secret with masked fields. Requires an all-scope read; the scoped, per-component view is the effective-secrets route. Gated by secret:read.

post.description: Seals a secret at an owner scope (a global secret needs an all-scoped grant). Fields are validated and encrypted against the type shape. Gated by secret:create.

delete.description: Removes a secret by id. Gated by secret:delete; read and delete scopes on the owner drive the 404 versus 403 split.

patch.description: Replaces the given field values on a secret, re-sealing secret fields. Only values change; name, type, and owner are fixed at creation. An omitted field keeps its value. Gated by secret:update.

post.description: Decrypts and returns a secret's field values for a clipboard copy, audited under the copy verb (distinct from an on-screen reveal). Same exposure and the same secret:reveal gate as reveal.
post.summary: Decrypt a secret for clipboard copy

post.description: Decrypts and returns a secret's field values, auditing the decrypt. Sensitive: gated by secret:reveal, which the viewer read floor does not carry, so only admin (secret:*) and owner may reveal.

get.description: Lists the systems the caller may read, each filtered to its scope subtree. Gated by system:read.

post.description: Creates a system, optionally under a parent (a root needs an all-scoped grant) and at a location. Gated by system:create.

delete.description: Deletes a system, refused while it still has child systems. Gated by system:delete; read and delete scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches a system by name within the caller's read scope. Out of scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by system:read.

patch.description: Patches a system's display_name or system_type. Gated by system:update; read and update scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Lists the tags bound directly on a system (not the resolved cascade). Gated by system:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a system. Gated by system:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a system

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a system. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by system:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a system

get.description: Lists the governed key vocabulary. Rides the tag:read floor.

post.description: Adds a key to the governed vocabulary. The name is normalized (a lowercase identifier). Gated by tag:create (all-scope, an admin action).

delete.description: Removes a key from the vocabulary, cascading its bindings. Gated by tag:delete (all-scope).

patch.description: Replaces a key's governance fields (applies_to, propagates); the name is fixed. Gated by tag:update (all-scope).

post.description: Removes the global binding for a key. Gated by tag:update (all-scope).
Binds a tenant-wide default value for a key at the global scope. Gated by tag:update (all-scope).

get.description: Lists every variable. Requires an all-scope read; the scoped, per-component view is the effective-variables route. Gated by variable:read.

post.description: Sets a variable at an owner scope (a global variable needs an all-scoped grant). The value is validated against value_type. Gated by variable:create.

delete.description: Removes a variable by id. Gated by variable:delete; read and delete scopes on the owner drive the 404 versus 403 split.

patch.description: Replaces a variable's value, validated against its fixed value_type. Only the value changes; name, type, and owner are fixed at creation. Gated by variable:update.
